summary: GH-100 implementation is approved for static package ref / lock semantics. The sample package now has `package-lock.json`, manifest/local registry/lifecycle metadata expose package lock identity, the stdlib validator checks package lock resolution, locked asset refs, and invalidation behavior, and the change introduces no dependencies, generated outputs, App install/update behavior, hosted registry behavior, Core Run Record/result envelope behavior, runtime behavior, Core/Harbor/App code, or write capability.
reviewed_validation_summary: Local validation passed on 2026-07-01 for `python3 tools/lode_validate_package.py sites/example/read-public-page --json`, `python3 tools/lode_validate_package.py sites/example/read-public-page --registry-index registry/local-packages.json --json`, `PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m py_compile tools/lode_validate_package.py`, package/Loom JSON syntax checks, `git diff --check`, `loom doctor --target . --json`, `loom verify --target . --json`, `loom fact-chain --target . --json`, `loom suite validate --target . --item GH-100 --json`, and `loom suite carrier validate --target . --item GH-100 --json`; both validator invocations returned status `passed` with no warnings and checked `package_lock`; `loom build --target . --item GH-100 --build-evidence .loom/specs/GH-100/build-evidence.json --json` was classified as a Loom build adapter gap after direct suite and carrier validation passed.

semantic_review_disposition.reason: Review binds the implementation head `65956788ae24247467685f9387b0e5d3e49b5096`; subsequent changes are limited to review/progress/status carriers and PR metadata, with no package lock asset, validator, package metadata, runtime, dependency, Core, Harbor, App, or external behavior changes.

follow_ups: GH-101 through GH-103 must cover sample read package closeout, Core fixture consumption, and deferred write guardrail.
